perturbation.original_text: Unless either party gives written notice of termination of this Agreement at least 60 days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any successive three-year term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive additional three-year terms; provided, however, that this Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the dissolution of the client.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless either party gives written notice of termination of this Agreement at least 60 days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any successive three-year term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive additional three-year terms; this automatic renewal is contingent on Agent's explicit written approval 30 days prior to renewal; provided, however, that this Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the dissolution of the client.
perturbation.explanation: The added condition requiring the Agent's 'explicit written approval' for automatic renewal introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement would renew automatically unless a termination notice was provided. Now, while it states it 'shall automatically renew,' it also requires Agent approval, effectively negating the automatic nature of the renewal process. This creates uncertainty about renewal conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term of Agreement. Agent's appointment hereunder shall commence on the next business day after the later of (i) the date hereof, or (ii) the date Agent has confirmed that Client's records have been converted to Agent's system (the "Effective Date"), and shall continue for three years thereafter (the "Initial Term"). Unless either party gives written notice of termination of this Agreement at least 60 days prior to the end of the Initial Term, or any successive three-year term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive additional three-year terms; this automatic renewal is contingent on Agent's explicit written approval 30 days prior to renewal; provided, however, that this Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the dissolution of the client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a contingency ('this automatic renewal is contingent on Agent's explicit written approval 30 days prior to renewal') to the automatic renewal clause. This directly contradicts the original assertion that the agreement 'shall automatically renew,' creating a structural flaw and in-text contradiction. The contradiction arises because one part of the agreement states the renewal is automatic, while another part introduces a condition (Agent's approval) that undermines the automatic nature of the renewal, as stated in the perturbation explanation and definition.

perturbation.original_text: All amounts owed to Agent hereunder are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Delinquent payments are subject to a late payment charge of one and one half percent (1.5%) per month commencing sixty (60) days from the invoice date. Client agrees to reimburse Agent for any attorney's fees and any other costs associated with collecting delinquent payments.
perturbation.changed_text: All amounts owed to Agent hereunder are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Delinquent payments are subject to a late payment charge of one and one half percent (1.5%) per month commencing sixty (60) days from the invoice date. Client agrees to reimburse Agent for any attorney's fees ONLY if it is approved by the Client's board associated with collecting delinquent payments.
perturbation.explanation: The added condition requiring the Client's board 'approval' for attorney's fees reimbursement introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, it states 'Client agrees to reimburse Agent for any attorney's fees,' which it indicates that the Client agrees to be liable for that cost, but now it needs approval from the board which may not be approved or delayed which could create uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Expenses. (a) Commencing on the Effective Date, Client shall compensate Agent for its services hereunder in accordance with the fee schedules listed in Exhibit B hereto. (b) All amounts owed to Agent hereunder are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Delinquent payments are subject to a late payment charge of one and one half percent (1.5%) per month commencing sixty (60) days from the invoice date. Client agrees to reimburse Agent for any attorney's fees ONLY if it is approved by the Client's board associated with collecting delinquent payments. (c) Client shall be charged for certain reasonable expenses advanced or incurred by Agent in connection with Agent's performance of its duties hereunder. Such charges include, but are not limited to, stationery and supplies, such as transfer sheets, dividend checks, envelopes, and paper stock, as well as any disbursements for telephone, mail insurance, electronic document creation and delivery, travel expenses for annual meetings, link-up charges from Automatic Data Processing Inc. and tape charges from The Depository Trust Company. While Agent endeavors to maintain such charges (both internal and external) at competitive rates, these charges will not, in all instances, reflect actual out-of-pocket costs, and in some instances may include handling charges to cover internal processing and use of Agent's billing systems. (d) With respect to any shareholder mailings processed by Agent, Client shall be charged postage as an out-of-pocket expense at postage rates that may not reflect all available or utilized postal discounts, such as presort or NCOA discounts. Client shall, at least one business day prior to mail date, provide immediately available funds sufficient to cover all postage due on such mailing. Any material shareholder mailing schedule changes, including, but not limited to, delays in delivering materials to Agent or changes in a mailing commencement date, may result in additional fees and/or expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Client agrees to reimburse Agent for any attorney's fees', which creates a direct obligation on the client. The changed text adds 'ONLY if it is approved by the Client's board', introducing a contingency that didn't exist before. This contradicts the original commitment and introduces uncertainty regarding whether the agent will actually be reimbursed. The Contradicted Text and Changed text are on the same location.

perturbation.original_text: Agent shall not be liable for any failures, delays or losses, arising directly or indirectly out of conditions beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, acts of government, exchange or market ruling, suspension of trading, work stoppages or labor disputes, civil disobedience, riots, rebellions, electrical or mechanical failure, computer hardware or software failure, communications facilities failures including telephone failure, war, terrorism, insurrection, fires, earthquakes, storms, floods, acts of God or similar occurrences.
perturbation.changed_text: Agent shall not be liable for any failures, delays or losses, arising directly or indirectly out of conditions beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, acts of government, exchange or market ruling, suspension of trading, work stoppages or labor disputes ONLY IF the agent provided 30 days notice of the situation or possible situation , civil disobedience, riots, rebellions, electrical or mechanical failure, computer hardware or software failure, communications facilities failures including telephone failure, war, terrorism, insurrection, fires, earthquakes, storms, floods, acts of God or similar occurrences.
perturbation.explanation: The added condition requiring 30 days prior notice introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, there was a broad allowance and exclusion for those causes. Now the Agent needs to give 30 days notice before that happens which is almost impossible which creates uncertainty about the causes, as it implies that some conditions beyond reasonable control might require 30-day advance warning, which is generally impossible with events like 'acts of God' or 'terrorism'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Force Majeure. Agent shall not be liable for any failures, delays or losses, arising directly or indirectly out of conditions beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, acts of government, exchange or market ruling, suspension of trading, work stoppages or labor disputes ONLY IF the agent provided 30 days notice of the situation or possible situation , civil disobedience, riots, rebellions, electrical or mechanical failure, computer hardware or software failure, communications facilities failures including telephone failure, war, terrorism, insurrection, fires, earthquakes, storms, floods, acts of God or similar occurrences.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a requirement for 30 days notice before invoking the force majeure clause, which directly contradicts the original intent of the clause. The original text allowed for immediate exemption from liability due to events beyond the agent's control. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed text and reinforces that change. The new condition makes the force majeure provision unenforceable for events like 'acts of God' or 'terrorism' where advance notice is impossible. This creates a strong contradiction, as the modified clause now imposes an obligation that cannot be met for many of the listed events, creating uncertainty and rendering the clause potentially useless. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as there are conflicting implications.
